mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0% signals labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 60% of months where UNRATE drops below 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and consumer price index compress real discount rates and reduce inflation uncertainty, jointly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate improving consumer confidence and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during periods of low interest rates.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects stronger aggregate supply capacity and operational efficiency, leading to higher corporate margins and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in retail sales and food services exceeding 5% signals weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS drops >5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved income growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in walk-forward testing (Sharpe ratio < 0.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, concurrent stabilization in consumer prices eliminates deflation fears and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL stabilization under ZLB conditions produces negative or zero 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations.
